name: Team Booker
subject: What you might not know
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CORY BOOKER FOR SENATE
body:
A global pandemic isn't stopping Republicans from trying to defeat Cory. |
#
Friends -- Last week was the candidate filing deadline in New Jersey and five -- yes five -- Republicans are on the ballot to run against Cory for his seat in the Senate. While much has changed over the course of these last few weeks, here's what hasn't: we absolutely need to keep up the fight to defend Cory's seat. Mitch McConnell and his dark-money network aren't taking any days off from cutting hefty checks to try and keep control of the Senate. From the very beginning of this campaign, Cory has refused to accept a dime from federal lobbyists, corporate PACs, or fossil fuel and pharma execs. We're doing things differently -- we're building a movement funded by and for the people. **But to do it, we need your help. If you can -- and only if you can -- will you make a donation of any amount to Cory's Senate campaign? This unprecedented global pandemic has meant fundamentally changing the way we do things. Our staff is working from home, in-person events, rallies, and fundraisers have been canceled, and we're shifting gears to be able to reach as many voters as possible remotely now through November.
But no matter what happens in the weeks to come, you can be sure that Cory will still be fighting every day to help our country overcome and heal from this public health crisis.
We don't expect the road to November to be an easy one as we all navigate this new normal. But if we stay focused and never lose sight of how high the stakes are, we can win this election and keep Cory in the Senate.

body:

---
| | Hoosier Republican,

I hope you and your loved ones are staying healthy while hunkering down at home and social distancing.

This coronavirus pandemic has affected so much of our daily lives, including how we campaign. Elections are moving forward — even if they have been delayed. Our primary was just moved from May 5 to June 2, and we still have a lot of work to do.

We need your help as we move our get-out-the-vote efforts online and on to the phones!

As things continue to change, voters need to know how and when they can vote. Did you know that all Indiana voters are now able to vote by mail in the primary?

We recently launched a new program that allows Indiana Republican Party volunteers to make calls to their fellow Hoosier voters from their homes.

**[Will you join us by making calls from your home?](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fc54ae8fc3&e=1acd9bb3d6)**

It is imperative that we support President Trump, Governor Holcomb, and all Republicans candidates in 2020.

Thanks for your help!

\- Kyle Hupfer

Chairman, Indiana Republican Party

name: Kevin Hern
subject: Celebrating the Risen Savior
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Hern For Congress
body:
Peter, This Easter Sunday looks quite a bit different than how any of us thought it would, just a few weeks ago. We thought we’d be going to church to celebrate the Risen Savior and gathering with extended family and loved ones afterwards. Instead, we are all staying home, doing our part to slow the spread of COVID-19. During these trying times, my family and I are looking more and more towards Jesus and remembering His promises to not abandon nor forsake us. 1 Peter 2:24 says, “He himself bore our sins' in his body on the cross, so that we might die to sins and live for righteousness; 'by his wounds you have been healed.'" I hope that you’re able to take some time on this blessed day to remember and reflect on the grace of the Lord. Let us all pray that we get through this crisis together and come out the other side stronger and better than ever.
God bless you! Sincerely, Congressman Kevin Hern | | [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/282e4263e04f74c17c94c760ea83e7c0/34143/dd80489b66d3cda4/351022bfd0ebd8adc3d6b838967d376c/34143) | [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/282e4263e04f74c17c94c760ea83e7c0/34143/dd80489b66d3cda4/cf598d1b3010a133c3d6b838967d376c/34143)

name: Jason Yates
subject: Make this Easter memorable
body:
| | He is not here; he has risen!
---
| | | _“He is not here; he has risen! Remember how he told you, while he was still with you in Galilee: ‘The Son of Man must be delivered over to the hands of sinners, be crucified and on the third day be raised again.’”_ \- Luke 24:6-7
---|---
This Easter is likely simpler than usual. Peter, you are probably not driving to attend your Easter church service or hosting a large Easter gathering; instead you and millions of other Christians are at home.
It’s in the quiet and simplicity of this Easter day, with fewer commitments, that we have an even greater opportunity to spend time in prayer, worship, and reflecting on the power of the resurrection and its life-changing meaning for each one of us.
1 Peter 1:3 says, “Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! In his great mercy he has given us new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.”
We pray this Easter is a special and memorable one for you, not because of the Coronavirus, but because of the meaningful time you spend celebrating and worshipping the Lord for His resurrection.
We rejoice together that He has risen!
**United. We Stand.**
Jason Yates
CEO

_"Why do you seek the living One among the dead? He is not here, but He has risen. Remember how He spoke to you while He was still in Galilee, saying that the Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men and be crucified, and the third day rise again." Luke 24:5-7._ Thank you, Lord Jesus for sacrificing yourself, overcoming death and for loving us enough to be our Guide and our Savior to show us the way to treat our fellow Humans, no matter what the cost. _Hallelujah, He is risen!!_ On behalf of our family, I want to wish you all a Happy Easter! While I hope you all stay safe and healthy, I also hope you find enjoyment in listening to part of one of the most beautiful Easter songs, "Christ the Lord is Risen Today," that I played on the video below.

VIDEO: Watch Janet's Easter Message
| | Peter,

I don't know where I read it, but this is a [source of encouragement](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=7e509677e9&e=795c6d9796) to me:

**"The streets may be empty, but so is the tomb."**

Jesus died for us on the cross so we could be redeemed for ours sins and now He is risen!

He's blessed us with so many things in this country and right now we are united on fighting this virus. You're part of the answer by changing your behavior and flattening the curve.

So let me just encourage you and wish each and every one of you a Happy Easter. And rejoice, He is risen!

God bless.

\--Janet

| | | "The angel said to the women, 'Do not be afraid, for I know that you are looking for Jesus, who was crucified. He is not here; He has risen, just as He said. Come and see the place where He lay.'"
-Matthew 28: 5-6

There is no doubt this Easter will be very different for most of us. My heart goes out to those who are experiencing disappointment over missing their traditional religious services or a favorite family gathering. My wife Suzanne and I are sad to be missing the LaTurner family Easter egg hunt that we do with my extended family in southeast Kansas every year.

Disappointments aside, Easter is still a time to celebrate life and hope as we reflect on the glorious gift of Christ's resurrection. I am grateful for my life, for my family, and the many other marvelous blessings God has given me.

May God bless you and your family this Easter weekend and always.

Jake LaTurner

name: Rob Portman
subject: Easter Blessings
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Portman for Senate Committee | [Privacy Policy](http://www
body:
Peter -- Easter this year will be quite a change for many families across Ohio, including at our house. Instead of a large gathering with family, Jane and I will be enjoying a nice Easter meal with each other and our dog Huckleberry. At noon we will be having a group Skype with our children and later with my brother and sister and Jane’s family. Instead of attending church for one of my favorite services, we will be participating in Easter services online. Our nation is on hold as the coronavirus pandemic is posing unprecedented challenges to our economy, closing businesses and schools, and testing our social safety nets in ways they’ve never been tested In our lifetimes. Congress passed the unprecedented Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security (CARES) Act rescue package last month, and I believe it was necessary to help families, workers, and employers large and small get through these difficult times. But while our nation faces many challenges, there’s also a lot to be grateful for. I’m grateful for the encouraging calls and emails, the many prayers, and the countless examples of Ohioans stepping up to help their neighbors and communities. Easter for me has always been about hope and renewal. This Easter, let us be hopeful for a renewal of the American spirit as we work to stop the spread of this virus. And let us remember God’s many blessings in our lives, including all the first responders and health care heroes. Let us commit together to renew the American spirit as we work to get our state and nation back on track. Blessings,
Rob Portman [](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=27Ax0UmWOX2J8J_QwK9Z6sYYV_ledEgoYeQO-o30kdJeTLiOkPFE0-_P396RlgQw&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20200412_easter&n=1)[](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=YqgukYA3Mrlri-dqG7I8Tgt5i9qzFHm-zEGuH8vKJ58&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20200412_easter&n=2)[

This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](dpwillis67@gmail.com). To stop receiving emails, [click here](https://www.robportman.com/unsubscribe?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20200412_easter&n=4).

name: Charles Booker
subject: Happy Easter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
Faith is what fuels us. Happy Easter, from my family to yours.
#
Coming from a family of ministers, I was always taught the power of faith. Faith is what fuels this campaign. Faith is what drives me to stand.
No matter the faith you practice, may today be a chance for us all to recommit to living to our highest ideals, and to let our love for one another inspire us to keep fighting for real change.
It pains me not to be able to gather with my church family to celebrate the Resurrection today, but it is critical for us all to take precautions to slow the spread of COVID-19. Caring for our neighbors is the embodiment of faith in action.
I know faith can move mountains, and nothing is impossible if we stand together. From my family to yours, I wish you a Happy Easter.
Standing with you in faith,
Charles

No matter the faith you practice, may today be a chance for us all to recommit to living to our highest ideals, and to let our love for one another inspire us to keep fighting for real change. It pains me not to be able to gather with my church family to celebrate the Resurrection today, but it is critical for us all to take precautions to slow the spread of COVID-19. subject: Fwd: [Sign on and forward] No water shutoffs during a pandemic
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Keep Al Green in Congress
body:
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/IlFr4nU83aAxo-aEi3aw7qTomk00dfBd7ZDLAJsd1ik=/B8ITZ0-31Z6W1HAwKrbbPlpbT0OstUujZv6Onu6VoGA=)
---
| Team, Water is a human right. And especially now, access to clean water is essential to containing COVID-19 and saving lives. **Yet, 2 million Americans are currently living without running water in their homes.** [**That 's why Al is calling on state and federal leaders to institute a moratorium on water shutoffs and restore services to all those who are currently without. Will you sign on to help make sure every American has access to clean running water during this global health crisis?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/IlFr4nU83aAxo-aEi3aw7qTomk00dfBd7ZDLAJsd1ik=/Bc0RIUmafMpoTqood8otcAI_yNEN56J1cYtUB_2EIe4=?source=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&referrer=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Falgreen.org%2F) | **[SIGN NOW& FORWARD TO A FRIEND](https://act.myngp.com/el/IlFr4nU83aAxo-aEi3aw7qTomk00dfBd7ZDLAJsd1ik=/sg5EJXv7tDYG_ueu3f89J7BShP8bX8bWz7Ew6uNndp0=?source=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&referrer=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Falgreen.org%2F)**
---
Thanks for speaking out, team. Your voice is needed as we work to get through this crisis together.
\-- Team Green

\-----PREVIOUS MESSAGE -----
Folks,
A couple of weeks ago, I asked you to share with me how COVID-19 is impacting you and your family. Some of the most poignant stories I heard were from folks who were worried about having essential utilities like water shut off because they couldn't afford to pay their bills.
**Right now, over 2 million Americans don 't have access to running water in their homes.** At a time when frequent handwashing is the CDC's number one recommendation to prevent the spread of COVID-19, that is simply unacceptable.
[**Will you join me in calling on state and national leaders to prevent and reverse all water shutoffs for the duration of this public health crisis, and then forward this email to a friend?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/IlFr4nU83aAxo-aEi3aw7qTomk00dfBd7ZDLAJsd1ik=/sg5EJXv7tDYG_ueu3f89J7BShP8bX8bWz7Ew6uNndp0=?source=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&referrer=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Falgreen.org%2F)
**[SIGN NOW& FORWARD TO A FRIEND](https://act.myngp.com/el/IlFr4nU83aAxo-aEi3aw7qTomk00dfBd7ZDLAJsd1ik=/sg5EJXv7tDYG_ueu3f89J7BShP8bX8bWz7Ew6uNndp0=?source=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&referrer=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Falgreen.org%2F)**
---
Several states have already committed to a moratorium on water shutoffs -- but it's not enough. We need nationwide legislation guaranteeing that no American will be without clean, safe water during this crisis, and that all homes currently without water will have services restored.
Folks, this pandemic has exposed many of the faults in our systems. Too many American families lack healthcare, savings, and even access to essential services like water. And now, our country's failures in caring for the most vulnerable among us is a public health concern that affects us all.
[**It 's time to take action. Will you sign your name next to mine and join me in calling on our elected leaders to prevent and reverse water shutoffs throughout the COVID-19 pandemic?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/IlFr4nU83aAxo-aEi3aw7qTomk00dfBd7ZDLAJsd1ik=/sg5EJXv7tDYG_ueu3f89J7BShP8bX8bWz7Ew6uNndp0=?source=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&referrer=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Falgreen.org%2F)
**[SIGN NOW& FORWARD TO A FRIEND](https://act.myngp.com/el/IlFr4nU83aAxo-aEi3aw7qTomk00dfBd7ZDLAJsd1ik=/sg5EJXv7tDYG_ueu3f89J7BShP8bX8bWz7Ew6uNndp0=?source=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&referrer=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Falgreen.org%2F)**
---
Thanks for speaking up,
Congressman Al Green

Hello

Over the past week, I've had many conversations with Iowans who've all been having the same thoughts cross their mind: Will coronavirus be a threat to the people I know and love? How will we know when this outbreak is over? Who can we turn to during our time of need?

Something I've noticed: Most of you haven't been worried for your own health but for for the health and safety of those near you. As Iowans in a time of crisis, we look out for one another before ourselves.

I too am concerned with the health and safety of my neighbors. It could be my uncle, who has many underlying conditions, or one of the thousands of Iowans I've gotten to know over this past year.

Iowa's economy has also been on my mind after hearing from farmers, bankers, store clerks, and waitresses who aren't sure if they'll be able to make it much farther. Whole industries could go belly up. For instance, Iowa's ethanol and biodiesel producers were already just hanging on. How will this slowdown affect their survival as the demand for all energy products has evaporated in a few weeks?

This past week, we celebrated the most significant series of events to take place on Earth — the death and rebirth of Jesus Christ. Through his death, in what seemed to be our darkest hour, Christ gave us new life and a new hope for what has yet to come.

Armed with our faith that our worst days are long behind us, we can get up, put one foot in front of the other, and work our way through anything. In Iowa, we keep on working hard like we have for generations. I believe that one day soon we will look back at this moment wiser and proud of how we responded to this challenge.

I have faith in you, my friends, and neighbors that we will overcome together.

May God continue to watch over you and bless our great country.

I appreciate your continued support.

subject: Three goals:
body:
The coronavirus crisis is urgent and a transformational moment for our nation. We can't wait until November to begin delivering solutions for people who are hurting right now. And our work doesn't end | | | [](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285257bf/45c30650/1f46c3637/66e965ed/4056805049/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
The coronavirus crisis is urgent and a transformational moment for our nation. We can't wait until November to begin delivering solutions for people who are hurting right now. And our work doesn't end in November. The solutions we advocate for now can and should have a lasting impact on our economy and our government.

America can come out of this crisis stronger than before -- but only if we rally around candidates like Elizabeth who support bold solutions, rather than temporary fixes and short-term deals.

**To that end, Warren Democrats will be guided by three goals:**
1. Help elect Democratic candidates who share Elizabeth's vision for big, structural change and support bold reform as a response to this crisis.

2. Help power the fight at the federal, state, and local level by organizing together to push for the solutions we need right now and for bold, popular grassroots economic and government reform in the months to come.

3. Mobilize grassroots supporters to ensure Elizabeth remains a powerful national voice and is re-elected to the Senate in 2024.

As we've already seen, this crisis has put a spotlight on a number of existing structural issues: how our federal government operates and who it works for, the fragility of an economy that is skewed against working people, and the importance of universal health care and paid leave not just in times of crisis, but in our everyday lives.

We need to fight for the pandemic relief measures we desperately need today, and to make sure we elect Democrats up and down the ballot to see this crisis through for working families.

And with your help, we're building a powerful organizing network to do just that.

**[Add your name to endorse the three goals of Warren Democrats and say you're in this fight. ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285257bf/45c30650/1f46c3637/66e965ef/4056805049/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTVExMVBfU0dOX0JTQ19DXzIwMjAwNDEyX1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/warren-democrats-join?source=EMQ11P_SGN_BSC_C_20200412_V1S1_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks,

Warren Democrats
